configPanel.aiUnconfigured: Not configured — fill in the fields below and save
configPanel.baseUrlHintAnthropic: — optional, for a relay/proxy; leave blank for the official API
configPanel.apiKeyHint: — leave blank to keep the existing key
configPanel.contextLenHint: — pick by model window; old turns auto-compact at half-full
configPanel.searchBackendFieldHint: — the search service the agent uses for the web

configPanel.searxngHint: — self-hosted or reachable instance (JSON API must be open)

configPanel.webTestTitle: Runs one real search with the SAVED settings — save changes before testing
configPanel.opt_iters0: Unlimited (still protected by the context cap)
configPanel.searchBackend_auto: Auto (use a key backend if a key is set, else DuckDuckGo)
configPanel.snapshotBody: Crawling needs a connection to the local Unreal project. The offline snapshot is read-only, so environment checks and crawl buttons are hidden.
configPanel.connecting: Connecting to the local viewer server…
configPanel.memberNoticeTitle: Other settings are managed by the admin
configPanel.memberNoticeBody: UE paths, crawling, and LLM settings are server-side and only the admin can change them.
configPanel.memberCrawlRunning: A crawl is currently in progress.

crawl.workmf.label: Crawl project MF
crawl.workmf.desc: Scans every project MaterialFunction under the given Content Route and refreshes only the signature index — for the node library / AI / export to resolve; does not write to the Files work area.
crawl.projectmat.label: Crawl project parent materials
crawl.projectmat.desc: Imports the parent materials under the "Parent-material Content Route" into the Files "Work" area; also pulls in the project MFs they reference so you can drill into the function graphs.
crawl.projectmat.refresh: Files tab · Work (parent materials + referenced MF)
crawl.export.label: Re-crawl node export
crawl.export.desc: Re-extracts each node's UE path / GUID and refreshes the agent-pack node export metadata. Only needed after a version bump.
crawl.enginemf.label: Re-crawl engine MF
crawl.enginemf.desc: Scans every official MaterialFunction under /Engine/ and refreshes the engine MF index. Only needed after a version bump.
crawl.compile.label: Compile plugin
crawl.compile.desc: Uses RunUAT BuildPlugin to compile the crawl plugin binary for the current OS (Windows .dll or macOS .dylib) into tools' compiled/. Needed on first use, or when an engine upgrade causes an ABI mismatch; the "Compiled plugin" check turns green afterward. (On Windows it updates the committed compiled/ directory, which may need a later git commit; on macOS it only touches the gitignored Binaries/Mac.)

teamPanel.title: Team mode
teamPanel.envLockedPrefix: Bind address is locked by the
teamPanel.envLockedSuffix: environment variable (Docker / script deploy); shown here for reference only.
teamPanel.shareUrls: Share these URLs with teammates (bound to {{host}}:{{port}}):
teamPanel.memberAgent: Let members use the AI assistant (each in their own private sessions, spending the server's shared LLM key). Crawls and node-DB edits stay admin-only.
teamPanel.allowRegistration: Allow self-registration (visitors can register on the login page; approve here. Default 50K tokens/day)
teamPanel.memberLock: Lock members' thinking level and web access (members' controls are grayed out and forced to the settings below; enforced server-side too).
teamPanel.secureCookieTeam: Secure cookie (enable behind an HTTPS reverse proxy; leave off on plain http LAN or you won't be able to log in).
teamPanel.secureCookieLocal: Secure cookie (only enable once an HTTPS reverse proxy is in place).
teamPanel.disableBtn: Disable team mode (back to local only)
teamPanel.disableConfirm: Disable team mode? The server will accept local connections only (accounts are kept and can be re-enabled later).
teamPanel.enableIntro: Once enabled the server rebinds to an external address (same port, no restart) and teammates log in with a username and password: the admin has full access; members can view graphs, import/export, and read announcements.
teamPanel.existingUsers: Existing team accounts detected — they are reused on enable, no need to recreate (log in with the original credentials).
teamPanel.adminAccountHint: — created on enable, to avoid a "expose first, race to register" window
teamPanel.passwordMismatch: The two passwords do not match
teamPanel.langHint: This is the team default; members can override it on their own device (it is not a hard lock).

agentChat.examplePromptEmissive: Build an emissive material so the object glows on its own
agentChat.examplePromptSnow: Build a snow material with a rough feel and crystalline sparkle
agentChat.examplePromptPbr: Build a basic PBR material with a metallic look and reflections
agentChat.cmdValidateLabel: Validate and fix the current graph
agentChat.cmdValidateText: Please validate the currently open graph and fix any problems.
agentChat.cmdExplainText: Please read the currently open graph and explain its structure and effect in plain language.
agentChat.cmdExportLabel: Copy the current graph to the clipboard
agentChat.cmdExportText: Please copy the currently open graph to the clipboard.
agentChat.cmdCompactText: Please use the compact_context tool to compact the conversation history.
agentChat.cmdLogText: Please use read_crawl_log to read the latest crawl result and summarize whether it succeeded and the key points.
agentChat.cmdHelpLabel: What can the AI do for me
agentChat.cmdHelpText: Please introduce what you can do for me: the available tools, which actions you can run directly versus only propose, and a suggested workflow.
agentChat.cmdCrawlmfLabel: Crawl the project MF index (reports back to the AI when done)
agentChat.crawlNote: This launches the UE editor and takes a few minutes; progress shows in the Config tab.
agentChat.dbEditCreated: Node DB added {{node}} (verified:false), index regenerated and passed the parity audit. Run a "node export" crawl next to fill in the metadata before you can export it to UE.
agentChat.dbEditUpdated: Node DB updated ({{node}}: {{keys}}), index regenerated and passed the parity audit.
agentChat.dbProposeModify: Agent proposes editing the node DB: {{node}} (UE {{ue}})
agentChat.dbCreateNotePre: The new node is stored as
agentChat.dbCreateNotePost: (export metadata does not yet exist); run a "node export" crawl to fill it in before it can be exported to UE.
agentChat.dbPublicNotePost: — it only accepts clean Epic / public UE data. After applying, the index is regenerated and a parity audit runs; any failure rolls back.
agentChat.dbPendingApproval: Submitted for admin approval — once approved it is written to the public DB and reported back here.
agentChat.deleteConfirm: Delete this conversation? Graph files already written are unaffected.
agentChat.crawlReportInstrSuccess: (For the AI) This is the crawl you requested earlier. Please continue your previous work and re-query the index if needed.
agentChat.crawlReportInstrFailure: (For the AI) This is the crawl you requested earlier. Please find the cause of failure from the log, explain it to the user in plain language, and give a fix.
agentChat.unconfiguredDesc: First set up the LLM provider and API key in the "AI assistant" section of the Config tab, then come back to use conversational material generation.
agentChat.crawlmfStarted: Started crawling the project MF index ({{root}}) — progress is in the Config tab, and it reports back to the AI automatically when done.
agentChat.regenTitle: Undo the last turn's file changes and regenerate the reply
agentChat.undoTitle: Undo the last step's file changes
agentChat.newChatTitle: Start a new conversation (the current one is kept in history)
agentChat.unsetPublicTitle: Unset Main Agent: members will no longer see this session
agentChat.setPublicTitle: Set as Main Agent: all members can watch this session live, read-only
agentChat.emptyTitle: Start a chat to generate UE materials
agentChat.emptySub: Describe the effect you want in plain language; the AI generates the node graph live, and you can undo any mistake anytime.
agentChat.imgNote: Sent with your next message (needs a vision model)
agentChat.quickCmdTitle: Quick commands (or type / in the input to open)
agentChat.inputPlaceholder: Describe the material effect you want… (type / for quick commands; you can paste images)
agentChat.thinkingLockedTitle: Thinking level is locked by the admin
agentChat.thinkingTitle: Model thinking level: higher means deeper reasoning but slower and more tokens
agentChat.webLockedTitle: Web search is locked by the admin
agentChat.webOnTitle: Web search: on — decides before replying whether to check the web for evidence. Click to turn off (the agent goes fully offline)
agentChat.webOffTitle: Web search: off — web_search / web_fetch are disabled and the agent won't go online. Click to turn on
agentChat.lockNoteTitle: Thinking level and the web toggle are set centrally by the admin
agentChat.inputHint: Enter to send · Shift+Enter for newline

login.errPasswordMismatch: The two passwords do not match
login.registerSub: Your request needs admin approval; it expires after 24h if unhandled.
login.registerSubmitted: Registration submitted — waiting for admin approval (within 24h). Once approved, sign in with this username and password.
login.setupSub: First launch of team mode: create the first admin account (you can add members later in the Config tab).
login.foot: Stay signed in for 7 days · Connection set by the server admin

bigGraphConfirm.title: Open large graph?
bigGraphConfirm.body: is a large material graph. Rendering every node at once may briefly stall the browser.

crawlDiagnosis.noMaterialsCause: UE launched successfully but no materials or Material Functions were found under the specified Content Route.
crawlDiagnosis.noMaterialsFix: Go to the Config tab, update the Content Route to the actual path in the UE Content Browser (e.g. /Game/G1/MaterialLibrary/Function or /Game/G1/MaterialLibrary/Material), then re-crawl.
crawlDiagnosis.pluginLoadCause: UE failed to load the UEMatExportMetadata plugin, usually because the plugin binary does not match the current custom engine build / Editor target.
crawlDiagnosis.pluginLoadFix: Run Invoke-NodeT3DMetadataMaintenance.ps1 -ForcePackage in a terminal to repackage the plugin against the configured EngineRoot. If it still fails, make sure the plugin was built with the custom engine, Editor target, and module dependencies your project actually uses.
crawlDiagnosis.shadowPluginCause: Your UE project contains a local copy of Plugins\UEMatExportMetadata that shadows the packaged plugin.
crawlDiagnosis.shadowPluginFix: Delete the Plugins\UEMatExportMetadata copy inside your project, then re-crawl.
crawlDiagnosis.packagedNotFoundCause: The compiled plugin could not be found.
crawlDiagnosis.packagedNotFoundFix: Run Invoke-NodeT3DMetadataMaintenance.ps1 -ForcePackage to package the plugin first.
crawlDiagnosis.buildPluginCause: Repackaging the plugin against your engine failed to compile (common with API changes between UE versions).
crawlDiagnosis.buildPluginFix: Check the BuildPlugin log. If there are API signature mismatches (e.g. GetInputsAndOutputs / EFunctionInputType), the tool maintainer needs to update the commandlet.
crawlDiagnosis.pathNotFoundCause: The project or engine path is not configured, or the file does not exist.
crawlDiagnosis.pathNotFoundFix: Go to the Config tab, confirm ProjectPath points to the .uproject file and EngineRoot points to the UnrealEngine root directory, save, then re-crawl.

graphDiagnostics.missingOutput: Missing MaterialOutput node (a Material must have exactly one).
graphDiagnostics.extraOutput: Extra MaterialOutput node (a Material can only have one output node).
graphDiagnostics.mfNoOutput: MaterialFunction is missing a FunctionOutput node (at least one is required).
graphDiagnostics.unknownType: Unknown node type "{{type}}" — not in the node DB, export cannot map it.
graphDiagnostics.unresolvedMf: MaterialFunctionCall "{{id}}" has no resolved pins — the MF is missing or its index has not been crawled.
graphDiagnostics.commentOverlap: Node "{{nodeId}}" belongs to multiple non-nested comments ({{commentList}}), causing a comment overlap.

compilePluginState.hintNeedPlatform: Requires Windows or macOS to compile the plugin
compilePluginState.hintNeedEngine: Enter a valid UE engine root above first
compilePluginState.hintBuild: Compile plugin binary for {{platform}} via RunUAT

nodeExplain.reservedMaterialOutput: MaterialOutput is the terminal node of every material graph — all other nodes ultimately connect here. It accepts standard PBR channels such as Base Color, Metallic, Roughness, and Normal, determining the final visual appearance of the surface.
nodeExplain.reservedFunctionInput: FunctionInput defines an external input parameter inside a MaterialFunction, allowing the calling material to pass in custom values and making the function reusable.
nodeExplain.reservedFunctionOutput: FunctionOutput defines an output inside a MaterialFunction, returning computed results to the calling material. Used in tandem with FunctionInput.
nodeExplain.reservedMaterialFunctionCall: MaterialFunctionCall lets you insert and reuse a pre-defined block of material logic (MaterialFunction) in the material graph. Its input/output pins are determined by the called MaterialFunction — think of it as a function call in code.

inspector.colorPickerHint: Quick color pick (0–1; for HDR > 1 use the number inputs)
inspector.issuesClickToLocate: Issues — click to locate on canvas
inspector.healthFootnote: A health check runs after every import or crawl. Resolve errors before exporting a snapshot.
inspector.loadFailedDesc: This file could not be loaded ({{count}} error(s))
inspector.aiExplainTitle: Ask AI to explain this node (open Agent chat)
inspector.askExplainPrompt: Please explain the node "{{id}}" (type {{type}}) in the current graph: what it does, its parameters, and whether the wiring is sound.

fileList.confirmLargeGraph: This graph has {{count}} nodes and may take a while to load. Open anyway?
fileList.emptyCrawlDesc: This section shows output from "Crawl project parent materials". Crawling parent materials also pulls in referenced project MFs; "Crawl project MFs" only refreshes the signature index and does not populate this section directly.
